title: Motion Graphs and Their Interpretation
content:
Motion Graphs and Their Interpretation

Graphs are powerful tools for visualizing and analyzing motion. The most common graphs in kinematics are position-time, velocity-time, and acceleration-time graphs.

Position-Time Graphs

The slope of a position-time graph gives the velocity. A straight line means constant velocity; a curve means changing velocity.

Velocity-Time Graphs

The slope of a velocity-time graph gives the acceleration. The area under the curve gives the displacement.

Acceleration-Time Graphs

The area under an acceleration-time graph gives the change in velocity.

Example Problem

Given a velocity-time graph with a constant value of 5 m/s for 10 seconds, what is the displacement?

  • Displacement = area under the graph = $5 imes 10 = 50$ m
